mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in industrial production indicate expanding manufacturing output and aggregate supply, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage April 2021 value 106.31 shows recovery from pandemic trough
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change vs 20-day forward SPX return remains below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative mean return in >50% of top-quintile activation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation rising above a 3% year-over-year threshold signals that the Federal Reserve may begin tightening monetary policy sooner than expected, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3.0% (approx index level 273 given base 265)
evidence_refs: fact:April 2021 CPIAUCSL vintage 266.832, +2.3% YoY from 260.8, nearing threshold
failure_condition: After trigger activation, the proportion of positive 20-day forward SPX returns exceeds 55% over any two-year period, or the average forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and a declining unemployment rate signals strong consumer demand combined with tightening labor market, indicating a robust but potentially overheated economy; historically this joint pattern correlates with positive near-term equity returns as earnings growth dominates concerns.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2021 RSAFS $619.9B near record high, UNRATE 6.1% declining rapidly
regime_hint: post-recession demand rebound with labor slack
failure_condition: The composite interaction z-score (RSAFS increase + UNRATE decrease) fails to achieve a Spearman rank correlation above 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero interest rate policy regime, rising industrial production is a strong positive signal because it confirms effective monetary accommodation is supporting real output growth without immediate tightening risk, leading to above-average equity gains over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% in April 2021, confirmed ZIRP regime
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, the rank IC of INDPRO monthly change vs 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 4-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative mean returns in more than one-third of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5% from an elevated level, it signals that the labor market is tightening rapidly, which historically precedes wage inflation and Federal Reserve tightening, leading to a negative impact on S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE April 2021 vintage 6.1% with rapid decline from April 2020 peak of 14.7%
failure_condition: After trigger activation, the proportion of negative 60-day forward SPX returns is less than 60% over any 5-year period, or the average forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales figures reflect robust consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings; in the current environment of post-pandemic reopening, positive retail sales surprises translate into immediate upward equity market moves within the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS April 2021 vintage $619.9B showing strong growth from previous year
failure_condition: The one-week forward return following above-median RSAFS readings is not significantly positive (mean return less than 0.1% or t-test p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with rapidly falling unemployment indicates a construction-driven demand boom that strains labor supply and materials, raising input costs and eroding profit margins, which historically leads to negative equity returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST March 2021 vintage 1739K units, near pre-pandemic highs; UNRATE still elevated
regime_hint: post-pandemic housing boom with labor tightness
failure_condition: The interaction signal (z-score of HOUST increase + UNRATE decrease) produces an information coefficient below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the composite signal does not predict negative returns in at least 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The first increase in the federal funds rate from the zero lower bound unambiguously signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle, which historically triggers a repricing of risk premiums and negative equity returns over the following three months as liquidity is withdrawn.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2021 FEDFUNDS at 0.07%, near zero since March 2020
failure_condition: After the first FOMC hike above 0.20%, the 60-day forward return on SPX is positive more than 45% of the time across all historical instances, or the average return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).
